mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, RAF, and VEGFR signaling to block proliferation and survival of FLT3‑ITD+ AML cells
additional_notes: Strong activity in FLT3‑ITD+ lines including MOLM13; reported IC50 ~3–20 nM; widely used off‑label in FLT3‑ITD AML; suggested points up to 300 nM with optional extension to 1 µM

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and multiple other kinases to suppress AML cell growth
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; approved for FLT3‑mutated AML; reported IC50 in FLT3‑ITD AML lines ~5–50 nM; range includes 1–300 nM and 1 µM

mechanism: Targets FLT3, KIT, PDGFR, and VEGFR to inhibit signaling pathways driving AML proliferation
additional_notes: Reported IC50 in AML cells ~20–200 nM; suggested concentrations from 1 nM to 3 µM

mechanism: Inhibits SRC/ABL family kinases and partially KIT/FLT3 signaling, reducing downstream survival signaling
additional_notes: Preclinical AML data; reported IC50 in AML lines ~10–300 nM; suggested range 1–300 nM and 1 µM

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL, KIT, and PDGFR to block oncogenic kinase signaling
additional_notes: MOLM13 is not classically imatinib‑sensitive; useful as negative/low‑effect control or in combinations; expected IC50 often >1–5 µM in non‑BCR‑ABL AML lines

mechanism: Inhibits BCR‑ABL and KIT with higher potency than imatinib, targeting kinase‑dependent AML subsets
additional_notes: Reported IC50 in KIT‑dependent lines ~10–100 nM; suggested range 1–300 nM and 1–3 µM

mechanism: Inhibits ALK, MET, and ROS1 signaling pathways implicated in some AML subsets
additional_notes: Reported IC50 in various leukemia lines ~0.1–2 µM; suggested test points 10–300 nM and 1–10 µM

mechanism: Selectively blocks BCL‑2 to promote apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑199; standard agent in AML combinations; highly active in many AML lines including MOLM13; reported IC50 ~1–50 nM in sensitive lines

mechanism: Inhibits anti‑apoptotic proteins BCL‑2 and BCL‑XL, enhancing apoptotic cell death
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑263; reported IC50 often ~0.1–1 µM in myeloid lines; suggested range 10–300 nM and 1–10 µM

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 to reduce JAK/STAT signaling involved in AML survival and microenvironmental signaling
additional_notes: Reported IC50 typically ~0.5–5 µM in myeloid cells; suggested range 0.3–30 µM

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK3, modulating JAK/STAT pathway activity in AML cells
additional_notes: Approved for rheumatoid arthritis; expected IC50 in AML lines likely low‑micromolar; suggested range 0.3–30 µM

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK), affecting survival signaling in some AML subsets
additional_notes: Reported IC50 in myeloid lines often ~1–10 µM; suggested range 0.3–30 µM; preclinical evidence of synergy with other AML agents

mechanism: Inhibits mTORC1 to suppress PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling and AML cell growth
additional_notes: Reported IC50 for mTOR signaling ~1–20 nM; growth inhibition often ~10–100 nM; suggested range 0.3–100 nM and 300 nM

mechanism: Rapamycin analogue that inhibits mTOR to reduce AML cell proliferation
additional_notes: Rapalog mTOR inhibitor; early clinical AML trials conducted; growth inhibition often in tens of nM; suggested range 0.3–300 nM and 1 µM

mechanism: Antagonizes dopaminergic signaling and functionally inhibits STAT5 and related survival pathways in leukemia cells
additional_notes: Reported IC50 in leukemia cells often ~1–10 µM; suggested range 0.3–30 µM

mechanism: Inhibits aldehyde dehydrogenase and, in the presence of copper, forms complexes that induce ROS and proteasome inhibition
additional_notes: Often tested with copper (e.g., CuCl2 0.5–1 µM); reported IC50 in AML lines typically ~0.1–1 µM with copper; suggested range 10–300 nM and 1–3 µM; specify copper conditions consistently

mechanism: Activates AMPK and inhibits mitochondrial complex I, disrupting energy metabolism in AML cells
additional_notes: Reported IC50 in leukemia lines often 1–10 mM; suggested range 0.3–30 mM; monitor osmolarity and pH

mechanism: Lysosomotropic agent that inhibits autophagic flux and lysosomal function, sensitizing AML cells
additional_notes: Chloroquine derivatives (chloroquine or hydroxychloroquine) used; reported IC50 in AML lines typically 5–30 µM; suggested range 1–100 µM

mechanism: Broad histone deacetylase inhibition leading to altered gene expression, differentiation, and apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also an antiepileptic drug; reported IC50 in AML lines usually 0.5–3 mM; suggested range 0.1–10 mM

mechanism: Inhibits the 26S proteasome, causing accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Reported IC50 in AML lines often ~5–50 nM; suggested range 0.5–100 nM; synergy reported with FLT3 inhibitors and HDAC inhibitors
